(formerly known as **Best Debut**) was an [Empire Award](/wiki/Empire_Award "Empire Award") presented annually by the British [film magazine](/wiki/Film_magazine "Film magazine") [*Empire*](/wiki/Empire_(film_magazine) "Empire (film magazine)") to honor a [director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director") with a breakthrough film or an [actor](/wiki/Actor "Actor") who has delivered a breakthrough performance while working within the [film industry](/wiki/Film_industry "Film industry"). The Empire Award for Best Newcomer was first introduced at the [1st Empire Awards](/wiki/1st_Empire_Awards "1st Empire Awards") ceremony in 1996 with [Bryan Singer](/wiki/Bryan_Singer "Bryan Singer") receiving the award for his direction of *[The Usual Suspects](/wiki/The_Usual_Suspects "The Usual Suspects")* and last presented at the [16th Empire Awards](/wiki/16th_Empire_Awards "16th Empire Awards") ceremony in 2011. Winners were voted by the readers of *Empire* magazine.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Former annual British film award

| Empire Award for Best Newcomer | |
| --- | --- |
| Awarded for | Best breakthrough film by a director or best breakthrough performance by an actor and actress |
| Country | United Kingdom |
| Presented by | [*Empire*](/wiki/Empire_(film_magazine) "Empire (film magazine)") magazine |
| First award | 1996 |
| Final award | 2011 |
| Currently held by | [Chloë Grace Moretz](/wiki/Chlo%C3%AB_Grace_Moretz "Chloë Grace Moretz") - *[Kick-Ass](/wiki/Kick-Ass_(film) "Kick-Ass (film)")* and *[Let Me In](/wiki/Let_Me_In_(film) "Let Me In (film)")* ([2011](/wiki/16th_Empire_Awards "16th Empire Awards")) |
| [Empire Awards](/wiki/Empire_Awards "Empire Awards") | |

The **Empire Award for Best Newcomer** (formerly known as **Best Debut**) was an [Empire Award](/wiki/Empire_Award "Empire Award") presented annually by the British [film magazine](/wiki/Film_magazine "Film magazine") [*Empire*](/wiki/Empire_(film_magazine) "Empire (film magazine)") to honor a [director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director") with a breakthrough film or an [actor](/wiki/Actor "Actor") who has delivered a breakthrough performance while working within the [film industry](/wiki/Film_industry "Film industry"). The Empire Award for Best Newcomer was first introduced at the [1st Empire Awards](/wiki/1st_Empire_Awards "1st Empire Awards") ceremony in 1996 with [Bryan Singer](/wiki/Bryan_Singer "Bryan Singer") receiving the award for his direction of *[The Usual Suspects](/wiki/The_Usual_Suspects "The Usual Suspects")* and last presented at the [16th Empire Awards](/wiki/16th_Empire_Awards "16th Empire Awards") ceremony in 2011. Winners were voted by the readers of *Empire* magazine.

Since its inception, the award has been given to two male directors, eight actors, seven actresses and one film crew. [Gemma Arterton](/wiki/Gemma_Arterton "Gemma Arterton") was the only individual to be nominated two times, at the [13th](/wiki/13th_Empire_Awards "13th Empire Awards") and [14th Empire Awards](/wiki/14th_Empire_Awards "14th Empire Awards") ceremonies in 2008 and 2009 respectively. Twice two awards were handed out at the same year. The first time was at the [5th Empire Awards](/wiki/5th_Empire_Awards "5th Empire Awards") ceremony in 2000 where there was a tie between [Carrie-Anne Moss](/wiki/Carrie-Anne_Moss "Carrie-Anne Moss") and the crew of *[East Is East](/wiki/East_Is_East_(1999_film) "East Is East (1999 film)")* and the second time was at the [12th Empire Awards](/wiki/12th_Empire_Awards "12th Empire Awards") ceremony in 2007 where the award was split to [Best Male](/wiki/Empire_Award_for_Best_Male_Newcomer "Empire Award for Best Male Newcomer") and [Best Female](/wiki/Empire_Award_for_Best_Female_Newcomer "Empire Award for Best Female Newcomer") newcomers. [Chloë Grace Moretz](/wiki/Chlo%C3%AB_Grace_Moretz "Chloë Grace Moretz") was the last winner in this category for her roles in *[Kick-Ass](/wiki/Kick-Ass_(film) "Kick-Ass (film)")* and *[Let Me In](/wiki/Let_Me_In_(film) "Let Me In (film)")*.

## Award history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Empire_Award_for_Best_Newcomer&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Award history")]

The award was named "Best Debut" between the [1st Empire Awards](/wiki/1st_Empire_Awards "1st Empire Awards") in 1996 and the [7th Empire Awards](/wiki/7th_Empire_Awards "7th Empire Awards") in 2002 and was changed to "Best Newcomer". In 2000, 2007 and 2012, the category was split to "Best Male Newcomer" and "Best Female Newcomer".

## Winners and nominees

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Empire_Award_for_Best_Newcomer&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Winners and nominees")]

In the list below, winners are listed first in **boldface**, followed by the other nominees. The number of the ceremony (1st, 2nd, etc.) appears in parentheses after the awards year, linked to the article (if any) on that ceremony.

### 1990s

| **1997  [(2nd)](/wiki/2nd_Empire_Awards "2nd Empire Awards")** | **[Ewen Bremner](/wiki/Ewen_Bremner "Ewen Bremner")** | ***[Trainspotting](/wiki/Trainspotting_(film) "Trainspotting (film)")*** | [[2]](#cite_note-2) |

### Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Empire_Award_for_Best_Newcomer&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-MaleDirectors_19-0)** Twice a team of two directors have been nominated for the award.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-Groups_20-0)** The nominations for the cast of *East Is East*, *The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring* and *Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone* are counted as group nominations.
